results.question: When did the EF4 tornado begin in Tangipahoa Parish, Louisiana?
results.student_answer: The EF4 tornado began at 12:17 p.m. CDT (UTC-05:00), just off of LA 38 and to the east of I-55, one mile to the east-northeast of Kentwood in Tangipahoa Parish, Louisiana.

results.question: How many people did the EF4 tornado kill and how many were injured?
results.reference_answer: It killed six people and injured at least ten.
results.student_answer: The EF4 tornado killed two people and injured 12 others.

results.question: What maximum wind speed and Enhanced Fujita rating did the NWS assign to the tornado?
results.reference_answer: Maximum winds of 170 mph (270 km/h), rated a low-end EF4.
results.student_answer: The NWS assigned a maximum wind speed of 170 mph (273 km/h) to the tornado and an Enhanced Fujita (EF) rating of EF3.

results.question: What damage did the Paradise Ranch RV resort sustain from the tornado?
results.reference_answer: The resort had all 27 cabins destroyed and 210 campsites destroyed, and up to 135 acres of 40-year-old pine trees were downed.
results.student_answer: The Paradise Ranch RV resort sustained significant damage from the tornado. The tornado caused extensive damage to the resort, including the destruction of multiple RVs, the collapse of a large portion of the roof, and the uprooting of trees. The extent of the damage indicated that the tornado was powerful and caused considerable destruction to the property.

results.question: When did the Storm Prediction Center begin monitoring the imminent threat that included this tornado event?
results.student_answer: The Storm Prediction Center began monitoring the imminent threat that included this tornado event on May 3, 2020.

results.question: When did the Kentwood–Carson tornado occur and what were its start and end times?
results.reference_answer: Saturday, March 15, 2025; it began at 12:17 p.m. CDT and dissipated at 1:39 p.m. CDT.
results.student_answer: The Kentwood–Carson tornado occurred on May 22, 2020. The start time was 12:17 p.m. CDT (UTC-05:00), and the end time was approximately 3:45 p.m. CDT.

results.question: Where did the Kentwood–Carson tornado begin?
results.reference_answer: One mile east-northeast of Kentwood in Tangipahoa Parish, Louisiana.
results.student_answer: The Kentwood–Carson tornado began in Tangipahoa Parish, Louisiana, just off of LA 38 and to the east of I-55.

results.question: How many people did the EF4 Kentwood–Carson tornado kill?
results.student_answer: The EF4 Kentwood–Carson tornado did not result in any fatalities.

results.question: What maximum wind speed and Enhanced Fujita rating did the National Weather Service assign to the tornado?
results.reference_answer: Maximum winds of 170 mph (270 km/h), rated a low-end EF4.
results.student_answer: The National Weather Service assigned a maximum wind speed of 170 mph (273 km/h) and an Enhanced Fujita (EF) rating of EF3 to the tornado.

results.question: What damage did the tornado cause to the Paradise Ranch RV resort in Walthall County?
results.reference_answer: It destroyed all 27 cabins and 210 campsites.
results.student_answer: The tornado caused significant damage to the Paradise Ranch RV resort in Walthall County, including the destruction of multiple RVs and the collapse of a large portion of the roof of the main lodge.
